decision_claim: Long-term package promotion is allowed only when trigger evidence proves the current transition stack is insufficient, or when owner-directed implementation is explicitly labeled and scoped. Autonomous promotion must select at most one package and must preserve build -> test -> contract/invariant -> hotspot verification.
decision_invariants: Do not equate watch with permission to implement.
Do not start more than one heavy LTP package in the same autonomous batch.
Do not classify owner-directed implementation as evidence-triggered implementation.
Do not weaken runtime-owned approval, containment, verification, rollback, evidence, or claim-drift semantics.
Do not introduce Temporal, OPA/Rego, event bus, object store, full observability, A2A gateway, or external signing without a selected LTP scope fence.

packages.name: orchestration-depth
packages.why_not_now: Current deterministic runtime flows, replay, timeout, and recovery are still maintainable without a workflow engine.
trigger_evidence_required: pause/resume/timeout/retry/compensation graph repeatedly exceeds local state-machine maintainability
workflow recovery defects repeat across sustained workload windows
full gate evidence shows local orchestration is the blocking risk

packages.name: policy-runtime-separation
packages.why_not_now: Local PolicyDecision, approval, and evidence surfaces remain sufficient; policy cardinality and audit pressure have not exceeded local maintainability.
trigger_evidence_required: policy count or ownership boundaries exceed local PolicyDecision maintainability
audit requirements need separately versioned policy evaluation
policy review burden becomes the primary delivery blocker

packages.name: data-plane-scaling
packages.why_not_now: SQLite/filesystem metadata, replay export, retention, rollback, and provenance cover the current pressure.
trigger_evidence_required: event throughput, replay retention, query latency, or artifact size exceeds current persistence path
multi-process service operation makes filesystem or SQLite metadata the blocking risk
release promotion requires object-store semantics beyond local artifacts

packages.name: multi-host-first-class
packages.why_not_now: Codex live continuity and one non-Codex degraded path are certified; broader first-class host productization remains demand-triggered.
trigger_evidence_required: non-Codex conformance parity is stable across real workloads
product demand requires deeper host-to-host collaboration
protocol boundary pressure cannot be handled by adapter conformance alone

packages.name: operations-hardening
packages.why_not_now: Sustained quick window and remediation evidence pass; full operations stack is not the primary risk yet.
trigger_evidence_required: sustained workload failures make recovery, SLO, alerting, or failure attribution the primary risk
doctor/operator remediation repeatedly cannot close posture failures
full gate evidence shows operational visibility is the blocking risk

packages.name: supply-chain-hardening
packages.why_not_now: Release/light-pack provenance floor is certified; external signing is not triggered until artifacts are externally consumed or team-shared.
trigger_evidence_required: runtime packages, control packs, or light packs are externally consumed
team-shared distribution requires signing or promotion policy
release claim evidence lacks sufficient provenance without external attestation

required_doc_refs.contains: Do not directly force the full heavy stack as the default route.

rollback_ref: git revert the autonomous LTP promotion policy, evaluator, tests, ADR, planning docs, backlog, seeds, claim-catalog entry, and evidence
